notes: Builds a two-stage HITL rubric-generation + LLM-judge framework and evaluates it empirically on 115 patches for 48 sanitizer bugs. Human-subjects signal is a rater-agreement/annotation study (authors act as raters; measures Fleiss/Cohen's kappa, inter-rater reliability, consensus labeling) rather than a recruited user study — a formal user study is explicitly deferred to future work. Includes ablations (golden vs draft vs free-form rubric vs GT patch) and thematic/qualitative analysis of rubric edits and judge/rater disagreements.
